findings.description: J. H. Sampson was the maternal grandfather of Jimmie Jewel Neal — that is, the father of her mother Martha J Wood, whose maiden surname was Sampson, not Wood. He was born about 1820 (birthplace recorded inconsistently as Georgia in the 1870 census and North Carolina in the 1880 census — not resolved by any record found).
supporting_sources: Arkansas, County Marriages, 1837-1957, entry for James W Neill and Martha A Sampson, married 3 January 1875, Nevada County, Arkansas — Martha's own marriage record, naming her maiden surname as Sampson and contradicting the compiled FamilySearch tree's unsourced claim that it was Wood.

United States, Census, 1880, entry for J. H. Sampson (head) and Louisa Sampson (wife), Caney Township, Nevada County, Arkansas — the relationship column explicitly records son Thomas R. Sampson (also found in the 1870 census of the same household) as son of the head and wife.

United States, Census, 1870, household of T Sampson, Ouachita County, Arkansas — same family three counties and ten years earlier; the household head's initial is indexed as 'T' here versus 'J. H.' in 1880, most likely a transcription misreading rather than a different person.

findings.description: Louisa Sampson was the maternal grandmother of Jimmie Jewel Neal — that is, the mother of Martha J Wood (maiden name Sampson) and wife of J. H. Sampson. She was born about 1820 in Georgia.
supporting_sources: United States, Census, 1880, entry for J. H. Sampson and Louisa Sampson, Caney Township, Nevada County, Arkansas — Louisa born about 1820 in Georgia, with the relationship column tying the household together as a family.
United States, Census, 1870, household of T Sampson (wife indexed only by initial 'S'), Ouachita County, Arkansas — same household ten years earlier.

Arkansas, County Marriages, 1837-1957, entry for James W Neill and Martha A Sampson, married 3 January 1875, Nevada County, Arkansas — establishes the family's maiden surname as Sampson, the basis for searching Nevada/Ouachita County Sampson households rather than Wood households.
